title: Deiregyne eriophora
intro: Deiregyne eriophora, also known as Eriophorine, is a species of orchid native to Central America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Deiregyne eriophora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Deiregyne eriophora is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Deiregyne eriophora is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a genus of orchids that is endemic to Mexico. More specifically, Deiregyne eriophora is a member of the tribe Cranichideae. The tribe Cranichideae is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae, which contains about 26 tribes of orchids. Deiregyne eriophora's classification helps to understand its evolutionary relationships and place in the plant kingdom.
